node_a:0:07a8c695.text: COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected people.

evidence_chunks.text:
COVID‑19 is caused by infection with a strain of coronavirus known as "severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2" (SARS-CoV-2).[85]
Transmission
Main article: Transmission of COVID-19
Transmission of COVID‑19

COVID-19 is mainly transmitted from person to person through inhaling air contaminated by droplets/aerosols and small airborne particles containing the virus. Infected people exhale those particles as they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing.[86][87][88][89] Transmission is most likely at closer range but can also occur over longer distances, particularly indoors.[86][90]

node_a:1:2567fdd7.text: Infected individuals exhale virus particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing.

node_a:3:e765d8d1.text: COVID-19 can spread by touching eyes, nose, or mouth after contact with contaminated surfaces.

evidence_chunks.text: COVID‑19 transmission occurs when infectious particles are breathed in or come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth. The risk is highest when people are in close proximity, but small airborne particles containing the virus can remain suspended in the air and travel over longer distances, particularly indoors. Transmission can also occur when people touch their eyes, nose, or mouth after touching surfaces or objects that have been contaminated by the virus. People remain contagious for up to 20 days and can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms.[16]

node_a:4:59b04548.text: People can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms and remain contagious for up to 20 days.

evidence_chunks.text: COVID‑19 transmission occurs when infectious particles are breathed in or come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth. Transmission can also occur when people touch their eyes, nose, or mouth after touching surfaces or objects that have been contaminated by the virus. People remain contagious for up to 20 days and can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms.[16]

node_b:0:81c35db2.text: COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk.

evidence_chunks.text:
Overview

Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), is an illness caused by a virus. The virus is called severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 or, more commonly, SARS-CoV-2. It started spreading at the end of 2019 and became a pandemic disease in 2020.

The virus that causes COVID-19 spreads between people in close contact. Most commonly, it spreads through the air in tiny droplets of fluid. Many people with COVID-19 have mild illness or no symptoms. But for older adults and people with certain medical conditions, COVID-19 can lead to the need for care in the hospital or death.

Sign up for free and stay up to date on research advancements, health tips, current health topics, and expertise on managing health. Click here for an email preview.
Email
Causes

COVID-19 is caused by infection with the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2).

The coronavirus spreads mainly from person to person, even from someone who is infected but has no symptoms. When people with COVID-19 cough, sneeze, breathe, sing or talk, their breath may contain the COVID-19 virus.

node_b:1:6d670fce.text: The virus can land directly on the face of a nearby person after a sneeze or cough, or be breathed in by others in close proximity or areas with low airflow.

evidence_chunks.text:
The coronavirus carried by the breath of a person with the infection can land directly on the face of a nearby person, after a sneeze or cough, for example. The droplets or particles could also be breathed in by other people if they are close together or in areas with low airflow.

It's possible to get COVID-19 more than once.

node_b:2:5ef977e0.text: Close contact, such as sitting next to someone or sharing a car, increases the risk of catching COVID-19.

evidence_chunks.text: Many factors affect your risk of catching the virus that causes COVID-19. How long you are in contact with the virus, whether the space has good airflow and the activities you're doing in the space all affect the risk. Also, your risk may change based on whether you or others wear masks, whether someone has COVID-19 symptoms, and how close you are to others. Close contact includes sitting next to and talking to other people, for example, or sharing a car or bedroom.

node_b:3:653e4be9.text: Risk factors include living with someone who has COVID-19 and spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow.

evidence_chunks.text:
The virus that causes COVID-19 can infect some pets. Cats, dogs, hamsters and ferrets have caught this coronavirus and had symptoms. It's rare for a person to get COVID-19 from a pet.
Risk factors

The main risk factors for COVID-19 are:

    Living with someone who has COVID-19.
    Spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow when the virus is spreading.
    Spending more than 30 minutes in close contact with someone who has COVID-19.

node_b:4:04360111.text: COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms.

Causes

COVID-19 is caused by infection with the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2).

The coronavirus spreads mainly from person to person, even from someone who is infected but has no symptoms. When people with COVID-19 cough, sneeze, breathe, sing or talk, their breath may contain the COVID-19 virus.

node_c:0:688ca7b0.text: COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing, or breathe.

evidence_chunks.text: The virus can spread from an infected person’s mouth or nose in small droplets when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing or breathe. These particles can range in size from larger respiratory droplets to smaller aerosols, and people can be contagious whether or not they are displaying symptoms.

node_c:1:da9ca351.text: The virus can spread via larger respiratory droplets and smaller aerosols, and people can be contagious without symptoms.

evidence_chunks.text: The virus can spread from an infected person’s mouth or nose in small droplets when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing or breathe. These particles can range in size from larger respiratory droplets to smaller aerosols, and people can be contagious whether or not they are displaying symptoms.

node_c:2:5668c8d6.text: COVID-19 can spread by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contacting contaminated surfaces.

evidence_chunks.text:
People may also become infected by touching their mouth, nose or eyes after touching surfaces contaminated with the virus. Experts are continuing their research into how COVID-19 spreads, and which situations are most risky.
Are the COVID-19 vaccines safe and effective?

node_c:3:add54803.text: Preventive measures include mask-wearing, hand washing, and avoiding close contact with symptomatic individuals.

evidence_chunks.text:
The use of a mask alone is not enough to stop infections and must be combined with frequent hand washing, covering sneezes and coughs, and avoiding close contact with anyone with cold or flu-like symptoms (coughing, sneezing, fever).

>> Read: COVID-19 and masks - Tips for families
How does COVID-19 affect children?

We are still learning how it affects children. We know it is possible for people of any age to be infected and transmit the virus, although older people and/or those with pre-existing medical conditions seem more likely to develop serious illness.

node_c:4:4e513b6d.text: Experts continue to research how COVID-19 spreads and identify high-risk situations.
